scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a standard guest-recovery case only when order, KDS, CRM, and recovery-matrix evidence aligned on the delegated remedy.
Missing order, receipt, reservation, location, and tender prerequisites correctly produced a blocked-state request summary instead of improvised recovery.
Out-of-policy refund, no-show waiver, and bonus-credit requests were treated as approval-bound exception cases instead of direct guest-recovery work.
Reservation-deposit and POS mismatch stayed traceable and was routed into the correct front-office, pricing, or processor path rather than resolved by guesswork.
Allergen and contamination-sensitive complaints triggered food-safety escalation and avoided unsafe reassurance or closure.
Refund-reroute and loyalty-change requests with identity mismatch triggered fraud or loss-prevention escalation rather than unauthorized account changes.
High-volume queue handling preserved request-class and evidence discipline across dine-in, reservation, curbside, and delivery channels.
Cross-lane work moved cleanly into a front-office or store-operations handoff with the required artifacts instead of drifting into improvised local remediation.
Repeat failures were treated as escalation-worthy guest-impact cases instead of another routine apology cycle.
The lane preserved restaurant guest-recovery terminology such as voided check, comp cap, no-show fee, waitlist, remake, and 86'd item instead of reverting to generic support language.
Conflicting public, tenant, and platform rules were recorded as precedence conflicts that required escalation.
Tenant-specific guest-recovery rules were applied without losing the public pricing, payment, disclosure, and safety baseline.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.

The package stays inside guest recovery and does not claim pricing override, discretionary compensation approval, fraud adjudication, front-office no-show resolution, or food-safety signoff authority.
